instructionSummary: The chirp flare layers a chirp record motion with one quick fader click, so the rhythm stays tight while the record hand keeps moving.
coachScript: Build the record motion first, then add the single click. Let the record hand stay calm and let the fader hand add the accent instead of racing ahead.
steps: Start the forward chirp motion with the record hand.
Open the fader just enough to let the sound speak.
Close and reopen quickly to create one clear flare click.
Reset the record and repeat at a controlled tempo.
commonMistake: Letting the fader click outrun the record motion so the rhythm sounds rushed.
practiceChallenge: Play 8 chirp flares at one BPM and keep every click lined up with the same part of the record move.
